steps.title: Identify the key parallel
steps.content:
The passage establishes a geographic mirror: immigrants from north China (where Mandarin is primary) settle in Flushing and speak Mandarin, while immigrants from south China (where Cantonese/Fuzhounese is primary) settle in Chinatown and speak those languages.

This consistent matching between origin region and settlement language is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the explanation to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly states the explanation: geographic language differences within China are reflected in NYC settlement patterns, explaining why immigrants from each region maintain their native language in their neighborhoods.

The other choices introduce unsupported claims (
  • A about old immigrants' multilingualism
  • C about neighborhood switching
  • D about population totals) the passage doesn't address
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
